summary: Tested unread notification count badge feature. Backend APIs working correctly (6/6 tests passed). Frontend badge displays correctly with count, proper styling (red bg, white text), and navigation to notifications page works. Minor issue: badge count doesn't refresh in real-time when marking notifications as read on the notifications page.

integration_issues.issue: CandidateNotifications component doesn't call refreshUnreadNotifications from DashboardContext after marking notification as read, so navbar badge count doesn't update until page refresh

action_items: OPTIONAL: Update CandidateNotifications.jsx to call refreshUnreadNotifications from DashboardContext after markAsRead() to update navbar badge in real-time
critical_code_review_comments: Backend implementation is solid - /unread-count endpoint correctly counts pending notifications
Frontend badge logic correctly shows '9+' for counts > 9 (line 983)
Badge styling is correct: red background, white text, rounded-full
refreshUnreadNotifications function is exposed in DashboardContext (line 562) but not used by CandidateNotifications component

success_rate.frontend: 90% - Core functionality works, minor real-time refresh issue

context_for_next_testing_agent: Notification badge feature is working. Badge appears on bell icon with correct count, shows '9+' for counts > 9, disappears when count is 0. Minor enhancement: CandidateNotifications.jsx should call refreshUnreadNotifications after marking notifications as read.

badge_appears_on_bell.evidence: Red badge with white text appears on notification bell when count > 0

badge_shows_correct_count.evidence: Badge shows numeric count (tested with count=1)

badge_shows_9_plus.evidence: Code at line 983 shows '9+' when unreadNotificationCount > 9

badge_disappears_when_zero.evidence: Code at line 981 only renders badge when unreadNotificationCount > 0

badge_refresh_on_read.evidence: Badge count resets to 0 on click (line 974), but doesn't refresh when marking individual notifications as read on notifications page
